chunk_summary: Guidance on supplementary feeding under ICDS and Mission Poshan, including nutrition norms, entitlements, and counselling for malnourished children, with specific energy and protein targets for recovery.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of ICDS and Mission Poshan in supplementary feeding for malnourished children.
Learn the daily energy and protein norms for children aged 6–72 months under the programme.
Identify factors affecting the effectiveness of supplementary feeding regimens.
Apply WHO's 'top-up' concept to optimize recovery in malnourished children.
key_facts: Supplementary feeding under ICDS is delivered for at least 300 days per year via anganwadi services.
Daily nutrition norms: 500 kcal and 12–15 g protein for children 6–72 months; 800 kcal and 20–25 g protein for severely malnourished children.
Supplementary feeding is provided as take-home rations (THR) or hot cooked meals (HCM).
The 2017 ICDS rules emphasize enhanced energy and protein provisions for malnourished children.
WHO's 'top-up' concept suggests supplementary feeding should contribute 40–60% of daily energy needs during recovery.
reference_quote: The 2017 supplementary nutrition rules under ICDS also describe enhanced provisions for malnourished children, including energy and protein targets for THR in younger children and added calories and protein in older preschool children on top of the standard meal.
document_key_topics: Supplementary feeding under ICDS and Mission Poshan
